mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market absorption, which boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings revisions, driving immediate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.3pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction starts and rising mortgage debt outstanding confirms a credit-fueled housing cycle that expands aggregate demand, lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95, supply chain bottlenecks clear and capacity utilization peaks, allowing firms to expand margins and pass cost shocks to consumers, which supports equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 when INDPRO remains above 95 for consecutive quarters, indicating mean reversion dominates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly proxies for corporate revenue expansion, and sustained positive growth rates mechanically increase discounted cash flow valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (p > 0.10) across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the consumer price index triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing central banks to signal tighter policy and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives short-term negative returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL jumps > 0.2pp, contradicting the inflation repricing channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing employment expansion, which validates earnings growth assumptions and sustains positive equity momentum over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 700 thousand units alongside rising mortgage debt, credit conditions are sufficiently loose to amplify the wealth effect from new construction, generating immediate positive equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where HOUST surpasses 700 and RSAFS expands simultaneously.
